evidence: Read lines 1-449. WorkloadCredential signs principal kind, tenant, subject/service identity, audience, and expiry at auth.rs:63-100; WorkloadAuthenticator verifies the signature before expiry/audience/tenant at auth.rs:214-267; middleware gates all privileged routes at auth.rs:324-339. LocalDevAuthenticator is unauthenticated at auth.rs:270-309, but the shipped binary confines that fallback to loopback and requires workload auth for public bind at crates/wsf-api/src/main.rs:100-145. No candidate promoted from this file.

Read lines 1-937. Router authentication is applied to every /v1 route at lib.rs:80-99; token tenant binding is centralized at lib.rs:270-288; issuance, verification, attenuation, seal, unseal, exchange, and receipt/export handlers were individually traced at lib.rs:327-745. Candidates review_wsf_front-001 through -009 preserve the independently reachable model-policy, delegation, revocation, credential-lifetime, and classification-control instances.

Read lines 1-247. TenantIssuancePolicy declares grantable roles, modes, max delegation depth, classification, budget, and model controls at policy.rs:50-75; role/model/mode helpers are at policy.rs:77-108; unknown tenants deny at policy.rs:111-177. Empty model allowlists mean unrestricted at policy.rs:72-87, supporting review_wsf_front-001. max_delegation_depth and max_classification are root controls for review_wsf_front-002 and -009.

Read lines 1-519. IssueTokenRequest carries tenant/subject/roles/budget/models at lib.rs:109-155; issue_token authenticates to OpenBao, loads the selected tenant, pseudonymizes subject, composes, and signs at lib.rs:217-240; compose_token copies request models and OpenBao classification into the token at lib.rs:301-354. The public bridge method is not itself a network endpoint, and the shipped API wrapper derives tenant/subject server-side at crates/wsf-api/src/lib.rs:417-428. Findings are therefore anchored to the reachable API policy bypasses, not to a bare public library sink.

Read lines 1-765. AWS exchange verifies then derives duration/policy before OpenBao and STS at lib.rs:185-233; the session policy intersects resource caveats and allowed actions and denies empty/wildcard scope at lib.rs:264-303; revocation is optional at construction and bypassed when absent at lib.rs:148-175 and 305-346; remaining lifetime is widened to the STS floor by clamp at lib.rs:349-361. Candidates review_wsf_front-007 and -008 preserve the independently reachable AWS exchange instances.
